mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where unemployment rises >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive rank IC over 24-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening real economic activity and higher capacity utilization, which translates into improved corporate profit margins and positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns in >50% of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds the 3% annual threshold, the Federal Reserve raises the federal funds rate to restore price stability, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: High inflation combined with rate hikes produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings capacity and dividend payouts, creating a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero or negative rank IC with 20-day forward returns across all rolling 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll additions exceeding 150,000 per month confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue growth that drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Months with payroll growth >150k yield negative forward returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production declines while retail sales rise indicates inventory drawdowns and supply chain bottlenecks, raising input costs and compressing profit margins to produce negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-RSAFS divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.
